question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been officially released by the BLS. <cite index="3-1,3-7,13-1,13-7">The Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) increased 0.3% in March on a seasonally adjusted basis</cite>. Since 0.3% is significantly less than the 0.8% threshold required for the market to resolve YES, the market will definitively resolve NO. This is not a probability estimation but a certainty based on official government data already released.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI increased only 0.3%
Market threshold is 0.8% - actual result is 0.5 percentage points below threshold
Data was released on April 10, 2026 at 8:30 AM ET as scheduled
No possibility of revision that would change the single-decimal result from 0.3% to above 0.8%
risks: None - the outcome is already determined by official government data
